fields.title: Port Codex apply_patch into tau and route by provider
fields.description: Tau currently exposes edit/write for all models. We want a Codex-style apply_patch file mutation tool available only when the active provider is openai or openai-codex, and those sessions should use apply_patch instead of edit/write.
fields.design: Port the apply_patch parser/applicator into extensions/tau as a local TypeScript implementation with no new dependencies. Register a new apply_patch tool in tau, preserve file mutation queue semantics and sandbox write approval behavior, and switch the active tool set at runtime based on ctx.model.provider. For openai/openai-codex sessions, active tools should include apply_patch and exclude edit/write; other providers keep edit/write and do not see apply_patch. Because pi custom tools are function-call based, the first slice should expose apply_patch as structured input (for example { input: string }) unless we explicitly decide to extend pi core for freeform grammar support. Subagent sessions must follow the same provider-based routing, so mode-agent tool allowlists and worker session setup need to stop re-enabling edit/write for openai/openai-codex workers.
fields.acceptance_criteria: OpenAI and openai-codex sessions expose apply_patch and do not expose edit/write. Non-OpenAI sessions expose edit/write and do not expose apply_patch. apply_patch performs add/update/delete/move operations using Codex patch syntax, fails fast on invalid patches, and respects tau sandbox approvals. Prompt/tool guidance no longer tells openai/openai-codex models to use edit/write. Main sessions and spawned workers behave consistently. Tests cover provider-based tool activation and representative patch application cases.
fields.close_reason: Implemented Codex-style apply_patch in tau, routed mutation tools by provider for main sessions and workers, and added tests for patch application and tool activation.
